title: rosa sempervirens var. prostrata
intro: Rosa sempervirens var. prostrata, also known as groundcover rose or trailing rose, is a low-growing, spreading shrub that belongs to the rose family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rosa sempervirens var. prostrata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, Rosa sempervirens var. prostrata can be consumed as a tea, used in infusions, or applied topically as a salve or oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
